market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

analysis.rewritten_desc: Welcome to Dan’s stylish Anox Services Apartments, an entire home perfectly suited for up to four guests. Located in a vibrant part of town, this bright, modern space offers a comfortable bedroom, a well‑equipped kitchen, and a relaxing living area where you can unwind after a day of exploring. With a nightly rate of £122, you’re paying roughly 30% less than the city’s median rate of £175 – a rare value that lets you enjoy premium comforts without the premium price. The apartment sleeps four, featuring two cozy beds, fresh linens, and ample storage for your belongings. While you’re here, take advantage of fast Wi‑Fi, a flat‑screen TV, and a full‑size fridge stocked for self‑catering meals. The building is secure, and you’ll find easy access to public transport, local cafés, shops, and cultural attractions within walking distance. With 81 positive reviews and a solid 4.56 rating, guests consistently praise the clean, quiet environment and the attentive host. Book now to secure one of the remaining 64 open nights in the next 90 days and experience a high‑quality stay at a price that’s hard to beat.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a bright, well‑lit image of the living area as the cover photo; it showcases the space’s size and style and immediately highlights the value guests get for £122 per night.
key_diagnosis: Update the title to emphasize the 30% price advantage (£122 vs £175 median) and the 4‑guest capacity – the current title provides no compelling signal.
Add a full, keyword‑rich description (200‑300 words) that includes the nightly rate, guest limit, and local attractions – the listing currently has no description.
List high‑value amenities such as outdoor space, dedicated parking, and entertainment options; none are currently shown, missing a key draw for price‑sensitive travelers.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your nightly rate is £122, about 30% below the city median of £175, yet your 90‑day occupancy is only 29.3%—there’s a clear opportunity to fill those open nights.
